channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Twenty-six Nigerian lawmakers defect to new parties ahead of elections
item.description: Today, the House of Representatives experience major political shake-up as 26 lawmakers officially defect from their original parties. Lawmakers from APC, PDP, Labour Party, NNPP, and other parties mo...

item.title: Tottenham’s high‑stakes De Zerbi appointment in a relegation crisis
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur’s impending appointment of Roberto De Zerbi is portrayed as a high‑risk, high‑reward gamble in the midst of a deep crisis. The Italian manager is far from a safe, short‑term option; ...

item.title: Trump Considers Ending US Military Campaign in Iran Despite Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: According to reports, US President Donald Trump has indicated he may conclude the US military campaign in Iran even if the strategically crucial Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Administration officia...

item.title: Considerations for Helping Adult Children with Housing Without Jeopardising Your Retirement
item.description: A 74-year-old single parent with a mortgage and around $1 million in home equity seeks advice on whether to sell their home to assist three adult children following broken marriages. One child is fina...

item.title: Severe Weather Highlights Weaknesses in South Africa's Disaster Preparedness
item.description: South Africa is facing increasingly frequent and severe floods and droughts, highlighting significant gaps in disaster preparedness and response. Despite the government declaring a national state of d...

item.title: Improving maternal health services in Nigeria through targeted regional strategies
item.description: Nigeria has one of the highest maternal mortality rates globally, with over 1,000 women dying per 100,000 live births. The problem is not uniform across the country, with northern regions experiencing...

item.title: Senegal enacts law increasing prison terms for same-sex relationships
item.description: Senegal President Bassirou Diomaye Faye don sign new law wey go double maximum punishment for same-sex relations, with offenders now fit face up to 10 years in prison. The law, wey the National Assemb...

item.title: Meghan McCain criticises Senator Lindsey Graham for visiting Disney World during political tensions
item.description: Meghan McCain, conservative commentator and daughter of the late Senator John McCain, publicly criticised Senator Lindsey Graham after he was spotted spending leisure time at Disney World amid a tense...

item.title: Government supports nine arts organisations with TZS 188.4 million for creative development
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has provided a grant of 188.4 million Tanzanian shillings to nine arts and cultural groups in recognition of their contributions to the growth of the nation’s creative secto...

item.title: Nigeria Urged to React After 800 Citizens Deported From Niger
item.description: Last Sunday, Nigerian authorities received about 800 citizens deported from the Republic of Niger through the Jibia-Magama border in Katsina State. The deportees, made up of 345 men and 185 women, cam...

item.title: Violence Erupts in Buffalo City Over Disputed Nigerian 'Coronation'
item.description: Violence broke out in Buffalo City, Eastern Cape, after protests erupted on Monday over the controversial 'coronation' of a Nigerian national, Solomon Egbonna Eziko, as the local 'king' of the Igbo tr...

item.title: Internal conflict at News Corp over reporting of high-profile child sexual abuse case
item.description: Two major News Corp mastheads have publicly clashed over the reporting of a high-profile child sexual abuse case. The dispute has highlighted tensions within the Murdoch-owned media empire, where busi...

item.title: Report Shows 47% of SA Wastewater Treatment Systems in Critical Condition
item.description: The Department of Water and Sanitation has revealed alarming findings in the Green Drop Report 2025, showing that 47% of South Africa’s wastewater treatment systems are now in a critical state. Minist...

item.title: Nigeria increases crude oil supply to Dangote Refinery amid high fuel prices
item.description: For May, the Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) don allocate seven crude oil cargoes to Dangote refinery, which is two more than the usual five cargoes they dey get before, according to two tr...

item.title: South Africans face steep fuel price hikes from Wednesday
item.description: The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources has confirmed major fuel price increases set to take effect from Wednesday. Motorists across South Africa will see the price of petrol rise by R3.06 p...

item.title: How Homeowners Can Maximise the Financial Value of Their Rooftops
item.description: For many Australians, rooftops have long been seen simply as protection from the sun and rain or a place for solar panels. However, recent data from the Australian Rooftop Valuation Report reveals tha...

item.title: Tension rises over redevelopment plans at Coco Beach Dar es Salaam
item.description: Coco Beach, one of Dar es Salaam’s most famous public beaches, has once again become a battleground of emotions and disagreements following new efforts to redevelop it. The renewed debate erupted when...

item.title: Farm Worker Killed by Co-Worker in General Santos City
item.description: Authorities in General Santos City are actively searching for a suspect in the killing of farm worker Bilmar Marco Danieles Sr. The victim was discovered dead on March 31, 2026, inside a deteriorated ...

item.title: Key Headlines on Middle East Conflict, Philippine Fuel Supply, and Space Exploration
item.description: Today’s news highlights major international and local developments. In Dubai, a Kuwaiti crude oil tanker was set ablaze following an attack by Iran, which also caused damage to its hull. Meanwhile, in...

item.title: Jozi FM Board Chair's Lottery Fraud Case Escalated to Specialised Commercial Crimes Unit
item.description: Rapitse Montsho, chairperson of Jozi FM's board, faces allegations of fraud and theft involving almost R35-million in National Lottery Commission grants. Although charges were provisionally withdrawn ...
